2 min read
7 hours ago

--

Every developer knows that database queries, complex calculations, and external API requests can significantly slow down an application. The simplest fix? Caching. While massive tools like Redis are great for enterprise-scale architecture, they often introduce unnecessary boilerplate and server overhead for smaller applications, microservices, or local utility scripts.
That’s why I built ⁠js-cache-shuttle⁠ – a zero-dependency, lightning-fast in-memory caching engine for JavaScript and Node.js that gets out of your way and gets the job done.
Why use js-cache-shuttle?
Zero Dependencies: Keeps your ⁠node_modules⁠ light and secure.
Time-to-Live (TTL) Support: Automatically expires and purges old data to prevent memory leaks.
Dead-Simple API: Learn how to use it in under 30 seconds.
Quick Setup
Getting started is as easy as running a single terminal command:
npm i js-cache-shuttleBasic Usage Examples
Whether you need to save a fast variable or manage auto-expiring temporary auth tokens, ⁠js-cache-shuttle⁠ handles it cleanly.
const CacheShuttle = require('js-cache-shuttle');
const shuttle = new CacheShuttle();// 1. Standard Caching
shuttle.set('user_101', { name: 'Alex', role: 'Developer' });
console.log(shuttle.get('user_101')); // { name: 'Alex', role: 'Developer' }
// 2. Caching with an expiration timer (TTL in milliseconds)
// Cache this token for exactly 5 seconds
shuttle.set('api_token', 'secret_xyz123', 5000);
// 3. Deleting and clearing
shuttle.delete('user_101');
shuttle.clear(); // Flushes the entire cache
Behind the Scenes
Under the hood, ⁠js-cache-shuttle⁠ leverages JavaScript’s native ⁠Map⁠ object for O(1) time complexity lookups. When a ⁠get()⁠ request hits an expired item, it self-cleanses from the memory map dynamically, ensuring your app stays highly optimized.
Wrap Up & Open Source
This library is open-source and ready for production updates. If you want to check out the source code, suggest a feature, or leave a star, head over to the project repository!
NPM: https://www.npmjs.com/package/js-cache-shuttle
GitHub: https://github.com/Kishan-gaiwala/cache-shuttle
